items.title: Penang establishes PIFC task force to position state as tech‑finance hub, white paper due 3Q, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — Penang will establish a Special Task Force Committee to continue efforts to set up the Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC), based on a “whole-of-state” approach. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the decision, made during the state executive council meeting yesterday, was a step to strengthen the state’s commitment to realising the initiative. “I will chair the committee, while the State Secretary (Datuk Seri Zulkifli Long) will serve as deputy chairman, with members including the state financial officer, state legal adviser, representatives from the state economic planning unit (BPEN), Invest Penang, the Northern Corridor Implementation Authority (NCIA), the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA), and representatives from the private sector.

items.title: Anwar hails Muslim-Hindu NGO peace effort, says it reflects national maturity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim welcomed the peace effort by non-governmental organisations representing the Muslim and Hindu communities, saying they reflect the maturity of the people in choosing the path of peace, rejecting division and upholding the spirit of mutual respect. Anwar said the peace effort embodies the true spirit of Malaysia, a country firmly grounded in the values of kindness, togetherness, and inter-racial respect. Malaysian Muslim Lawyers Association president Datuk Zainul Rijal Abu Bakar said the dialogue provided a platform for both communities to engage directly, free from external influence.

items.title: Petronas Chemicals shares rally to one-year high on late buying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Shares of Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) jumped nearly 11 per cent in the final trading hour, as late buying pushed the stock to a more than one-year high. The shares were bought in two transactions comprising 3.6 million shares and another three million shares through Citigroup Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd. Following the purchases, EPF's direct shareholding in PetChem rose to 1.004 billion shares, representing a 12.56 per cent stake.

items.title: Samsung Elec plans over US$73bil investment in 2026 to lead in AI chip sector
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics said on Thursday that it plans to invest more than 110 trillion won (US$73.24 billion) this year in research and development and facilitiesin a bid to lead the semiconductor industry in artificial intelligence. Last year, Samsung, which is the world's biggest memory chip producer, said it invested a total of 90.4 trillion won, including 52.7 trillion won in capital expenditure and 37.7 trillion won in research and development. Samsung added that it plans to pay 9.8 trillion won in regular dividends for 2026.

items.title: Couple killed in head-on crash after car enters opposite lane
items.summary: Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the victims were travelling in a Nissan Sentra driven by a 26-year-old man. He said the car veered into the opposite lane before colliding with a Proton Saga carrying four siblings. The Nissan Sentra was carrying a couple, including a 25-year-old woman.

items.title: 'Kara Satukan Rasa' brings Ramadan joy to Rumah Titian Kaseh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kara Malaysia brought the spirit of togetherness this Ramadan through its 'Kara Satukan Rasa' campaign, hosting a meaningful celebration with 50 orphans and 10 single mothers at Rumah Titian Kaseh. (From left) Rumah Titian Kaseh representative Fadilah Muhammad Shaari, Kara Marketing Malaysia director Susanto and UtusanTV chief editor Metra Syahril Mohamed pouring Kara Coconut Cream into the bubur lambuk pot during the Kara Satukan Rasa Bubur Lambuk Distribution Ceremony at Rumah Titian Kaseh, Titiwangsa. - File pic credit (Kara Malaysia) As part of the programme, Kara team members came together to cook bubur lambuk, a Ramadan staple often prepared in large quantities and shared with the community.

items.title: Bad weather expected to put damper on Raya 'balik kampung' trips
items.summary: The department said the warning involves Kedah, Perak, Pahang, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as parts of Sarawak and Sabah. In Selangor, the affected areas are Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat and Sepang. Also affected are Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.

items.title: Couple killed in crash after car veers into opposite lane in Kota Tinggi, four others injured
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 19 — A couple were killed in an accident involving two vehicles at kilometre 77.3 of the Johor Bahru–Mersing road near Kota Tinggi this morning. Kota Tinggi district police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the victims were travelling in a Nissan Sentra, which collided with a Proton Saga carrying four people in the incident at about 9.45 am. “The victims, who were travelling from Mersing towards Johor Bahru, are believed to have entered the opposite lane and collided with the Proton Saga, which was travelling from Pasir Gudang to Kuantan,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Online scams cost Kota Kinabalu victims RM11.1m in nearly 3 months
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Online fraud cases in Kota Kinabalu caused losses of RM11.12 million from 214 cases recorded between January and March 15, police said. Acting district police chief Superintendent Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said the cases formed part of 290 commercial crime investigations opened by the Commercial Crime Investigation Division, with total losses amounting to RM14.628 million. He said 46 cases involved e-commerce fraud, particularly vehicle transactions, resulting in losses of RM328,888.

items.title: Kota Kinabalu police warn of vehicle scam tactics as online fraud losses hit RM11.12m
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Kota Kinabalu District Police Headquarters recorded losses totaling RM11.12 million from 214 online fraud cases between January and March 15. Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said these cases form part of the 290 commercial crime investigations opened by the Commercial Crime Investigation Division, with total losses amounting to RM14.628 million. He added that, besides online scams, 46 cases involved e-commerce fraud, particularly vehicle transactions, resulting in losses of RM328,888.

items.title: US expands visa bond rule to 12 more countries
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States (US) said on Wednesday it is expanding its visa bond programme to 12 additional countries, reported German Press Agency (dpa). From April 2, travellers from a total of 50 countries will be required to pay a deposit of US$15,000 upon entry to the US, which the government says is intended to prevent visitors from overstaying their visas. Visitors from countries in the so-called Visa Waiver Programme (VWP) are not affected, according to earlier information.

items.title: UK wages rise at slowest pace in over five years
items.summary: LONDON: British wages, excluding bonuses, rose at their slowest pace since late 2020 in the three months to January, according to official data published on Thursday which also suggested a weakening in employment might have bottomed out. Regular earnings rose by 3.8 per cent in the November-to-January period, the slowest increase since the three months to November 2020 and down from 4.1 per cent in the final quarter of 2025, the Office for National Statistics said. The number of people in payrolled employment rose by a provisional estimate of 20,000 people between February and January.

items.title: Madani govt ensuring sufficient supply of basic items, public urged not to panic buy, says Mustapha Sakmud
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Madani Government will continue striving to ensure that the basic needs of the people are sufficient, especially in Sabah and Sarawak, in the face of current economic challenges, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Seri Mustapha Sakmud. He said the measures included maintaining fuel prices and ensuring the supply of basic goods remained stable so that the people would not be burdened by the rising cost of living. But at the same time, the Madani Government will try to ensure our basic needs are sufficient.

items.title: Evening commute alert: Thunderstorms to hit KL, Putrajaya, Selangor, multiple other states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an urgent weather advisory for the Klang Valley and several other states, warning of heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 6pm today. According to the alert issued at 3.50 pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat, and Sepang. In the northern and central regions, residents in Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah should prepare for adverse weather, alongside a significant portion of Perak, specifically Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.

items.title: Envacgen becomes Vietnam's first EV-A71 vaccine, paving way for Southeast Asia expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Medigen Vaccine Biologics Corp (MVC) and French pharmaceutical company Substipharm Biologics announced that their enterovirus 71 (EV-A71) vaccine, Envacgen, has received marketing authorisation from the Drug Administration of Vietnam (DAV). The launch of Envacgen in Vietnam not only addresses a critical public health need but also underscores the potential for innovative vaccine solutions to reshape paediatric care across Southeast Asia. This makes regional approvals like Vietnam's Envacgen an important milestone for potential Southeast Asia rollout, including Malaysia.

items.title: Cabinet ministers extend Ugadi greetings, highlight importance of unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several cabinet ministers have extended their Ugadi greetings today, urging the nation's diverse communities to continue reinforcing unity and harmony. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the Ugadi festival marked not only the start of a new year, but also a period of hope, renewal, and the beginning of a fresher, more meaningful chapter in life. Also extending her greetings was Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, who wished a joyful Ugadi and a happy new year to all her Telugu friends.

items.title: 25,000 chickens on sale at RM5 each in Masjid Tanah constituency
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A total of 25,000 fresh chickens were sold at RM5 each at Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency today in conjunction with the celebration of three years of the administration of Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh on March 31. In a statement, the Media and Communications Unit of the Melaka Chief Minister's Office announced that the initiative is also aimed at helping the people, especially residents of Masjid Tanah constituency, prepare for Aidilfitri, as well as to ease the cost of living. The statement said the chicken sales programme was held at the offices of the State Legislative Assembly Development and Coordination Committee (Japerun) of the five state constituencies under the Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency, namely Tanjung Bidara, Ayer Limau, Taboh Naning, Kuala Linggi and Lendu, starting from 8 am to 12 noon today.

items.title: PAAB establishes sustainable Islamic finance framework with blue finance elements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pengurusan Aset Air Bhd (PAAB) has established Malaysia's first sustainable Islamic finance framework that incorporates blue finance elements, with Maybank Investment Bank Bhd (Maybank IB) serving as the sole sustainability structuring adviser. In a joint statement today, PAAB and Maybank IB said the framework marks a significant milestone as the first in Malaysia to incorporate blue finance elements within an Islamic finance structure. "The framework allows PAAB to undertake blue instruments such as Blue Sukuk and other Islamic financings in adherence with the Guidelines for Blue Finance Version 2.0 developed by the International Finance Corporation in 2025, as well as the Bonds to Finance the Sustainable Blue Economy - A Practitioner's Guide published by the International Capital Market Association in 2023.

items.title: 25,000 Chickens on sale at RM5 each in Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A total of 25,000 fresh chickens were sold at RM5 each at Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency today in conjunction with the celebration of three years of the administration of Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh on March 31. In a statement, the Media and Communications Unit of the Melaka Chief Minister's Office announced that the initiative is also aimed at helping the people, especially residents of Masjid Tanah constituency, prepare for Aidilfitri, as well as to ease the cost of living. The statement said the chicken sales programme was held at the offices of the State Legislative Assembly Development and Coordination Committee (Japerun) of the five state constituencies under the Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency, namely Tanjung Bidara, Ayer Limau, Taboh Naning, Kuala Linggi and Lendu, starting from 8am to 12pm today.

items.title: Yinson Q4 profit falls 64.5pct on absence of tax credit, lower EPCIC contributions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd's net profit for the fourth quarter ended Jan 31, 2026 (Q4 FY26), fell 64.5 per cent to RM228 million from RM643 million a year ago. Earnings per share (EPS) fell 76.6 per cent to 4.70 sen from 20.10 sen a year ago. For the full financial year, Yinson's net profit declined 45.4 per cent to RM683 million from RM1.25 billion, while revenue slipped 28.5 per cent to RM5.44 billion from RM7.61 billion previously.

items.title: Gas producer Energean halts Israel 2026 outlook as Mideast war shuts production
items.summary: Reuters LONDON: Eastern Mediterranean-focused gas producer Energean on Thursday suspended its 2026 outlook for Israel after the Middle East conflict forced the shutdown of its production vessel that serves multiple Israeli fields. Growing regional tensions triggered precautionary shutdowns of key oil and gas facilities across the Middle East, including Qatar's LNG operations, Israeli offshore fields, and production sites in Iraqi Kurdistan. Energean also reported an annual adjusted core profit of US$1.12 billion, down from US$1.16 billion a year earlier.

items.title: Biscuit maker sleeps three hours a day to meet Raya demand
items.summary: Aminah Akmarina Osman, 40, said she and six workers have extended their operations until 3am to cope with increased demand. She said orders had risen significantly compared with last year, when she produced about 4,000 jars. She said orders are not prepared too early to ensure freshness.

items.title: IGP defends handcuffing Zamri Vinoth, says procedure follows Sedition Act requirements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said Zamri Vinoth was brought to court in handcuffs on Tuesday in accordance with legal procedures and regulations. He said Zamri’s initial arrest involved a case under Section 505 of the Penal Code, and he is also being investigated under the Sedition Act 1948, which requires a different procedural approach. Zamri, 42, whose full name is Muhammad Zamri Vinoth Kalimuthu, was charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court; Arun, 56, whose full name is Arumugam Dorasamy, was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court; while e-hailing driver P. Mahendra Bhoopaty, 53, faced similar charges at the Seremban Magistrate’s Court.

items.title: IGP: Businessman Victor Chin linked to money laundering ‘matters’, currently abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Police have linked businessman Victor Chin, a central figure in the ongoing “corporate mafia” probe, to activities under the Anti-Money Laundering Act (AMLA), Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. The IGP also disclosed that Chin, who has been openly critical of the investigation against him, is currently abroad as authorities seek international cooperation to advance their investigation, The Star reported. Separately, the IGP also commented on the police investigation into the international news agency Bloomberg, stating that several persons of interest in that case are also overseas.

items.title: Thailand's new parliament elects Anutin as PM
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's new parliament on Thursday elected Anutin Charnvirakul as prime minister, keeping the conservative in the top office after his party routed its election rivals. Anutin received 293 votes from newly seated lawmakers to win the premiership, with his progressive rival Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut gaining 119 and 86 MPs abstaining, Sophon said. The third-placed Pheu Thai party of jailed former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra agreed to join Anutin in a coalition alongside 14 smaller parties, and parliament anointed him on Thursday.

items.title: Retail reimagined: CityWalk and rise of AI-driven consumer spaces
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Retail Group Malaysia (RGM) has maintained its projection of 4.0 per cent growth in retail sales for 2026, although it cautioned that the outlook does not factor in potential cost-of-living pressures arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Jason Tan, an expert in repositioning and curating retail assets across the Klang Valley, said retail performance is driven by how well the concept aligns with the needs of the catchment. Drawing from projects such as 163 Retail Park, The Campus Ampang, and Central i-City Mall, he said that curated, experience-led retail formats can outperform larger, generic malls.

items.title: Friends reunite every Ramadan to sell jar-roasted chickens
items.summary: KOTA BARU: For the past 14 years, three friends have drawn customers to their stall at Pintu Geng here with their ayam tempayan (chicken roasted inside large earthen jars). Using a traditional method that relies on heat and steam trapped inside the jars, the trio sold more than 100 chickens a day during Ramadan. Mohd Deraman Husin, 51, who works at the canteen of Sekolah Rendah Sri Kota, said the business began as a small venture among friends.

items.title: Alif Ahmad ordered to pay RM130,000 over defamatory posts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Shah Alam High Court today ordered social media activist Ahmad Aliff A S Ahmad Shariffuddin, better known as Alif Ahmad, to pay RM130,000 in damages to two lawyers for publishing defamatory statements on social media. The court awarded her RM80,000 in global damages and RM10,000 in costs. Ahmad Aliff's counterclaim against both lawyers was dismissed.

items.title: Swatch CEO concerned about surge in value of Swiss franc
items.summary: ZURICH: Swatch Group chief executive officer Nick Hayek has expressed concern about the strength of the Swiss franc, Swiss broadcaster SRF reported, saying the safe-haven currency's appreciation was putting domestic industry at risk. Hayek, whose company owns watch brands including Longines, Omega and Tissot, accused the Swiss National Bank of not responding to the appreciation for fear of being branded a currency manipulator by Washington. "We have nothing against a strong Swiss franc," Hayek told SRF.

items.title: Batik Air starts daily Penang–Johor Bahru flights
items.summary: The airline said the encouraging response from customers highlighted the growing need for more direct domestic routes that bypass Kuala Lumpur. Penang International Airport recorded 741,183 passenger movements in January, making it one of Malaysia's busiest aviation hubs after Kuala Lumpur International Airport. Batik Air chief executive officer Datuk Chandran Rama Muthy said the new service reflects the airline's commitment to strengthening Malaysia's domestic air network in response to evolving travel patterns.

items.title: Chow rebuts claims of being out of touch with the community
items.summary: Responding to criticism that he should personally verify issues, such as the recent controversy over quit rent hikes instead of relying on official briefings, Chow said such assertions were inaccurate. Elaborating, Chow said the ongoing Jelajah Ramadan had taken him to all five districts in Penang, enabling direct engagement with residents and local communities. Chow said such outreach efforts were not seasonal, noting that his office organised Jelajah programmes throughout the year.

items.title: Cabinet ministers extend Ugadi greetings, call for unity and harmony
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Several Cabinet ministers today extended their Ugadi greetings, urging the nation’s diverse communities to continue reinforcing unity and harmony. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the Ugadi festival marks not only the start of a new year, but also a period of hope, renewal, and the beginning of a fresher, more meaningful chapter in life. Also extending her greetings was Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, who wished a joyful Ugadi and a happy new year to all her Telugu friends.

items.title: Hong Kong national among four held in Bukit Damansara gambling raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four people, including a Hong Kong national, were arrested after police busted a syndicate organising "mini casino" gambling under the guise of a sports event organising company. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said those arrested were a Hong Kong male national and three others aged between 27 and 49. He said investigations revealed that the syndicate was involved in managing, organising and promoting "mini casino" gambling.

items.title: Foreign investors dump Japanese stocks on Iran war jitters
items.summary: Foreigners sold about 1.77 trillion yen (US$11.09 billion) worth of Japanese stocks in their biggest weekly net sales since Sept 13, 2025, snapping an 11-week buying streak, data from Japan's Finance Ministry showed. Overseas investors bought a net 1.19 trillion yen of Japanese long-term bonds last week, reversing the prior week's net sales of 963.6 billion yen. They remained net sellers of short-term Japanese bonds for a second consecutive week, offloading 952.6 billion yen.

items.title: King lauds security forces, urges public to prioritise safety this Aidilfitri following audience with Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — His Majesty, Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia has expressed his appreciation of the security forces and enforcement agencies who remain committed to carrying out their trust and responsibilities during the Aidilfitri celebrations. In a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook today, His Majesty highly praised the sacrifices of security and enforcement personnel who remain on duty to ensure that peace, public order and the well-being of the people are maintained, even though other communities are celebrating Aidilfitri with their families. Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim granted an audience to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Tunku, here today.

items.title: BOJ leaves benchmark interest rate unchanged amid Iran war
items.summary: TOKYO: The Bank of Japan kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at around 0.75 per cent at the end of its two-day policy meeting on Thursday as it gauges the impact of the Iran conflict and surging crude oil prices on the Japanese economy, Kyodo News Agency reported. The bank held the key rate steady for the second straight meeting after raising it to its highest level in 30 years at its December meeting. Soaring crude oil prices will likely push up the prices of a wide range of products from gasoline to plastics, while electricity and gas bills could also rise if liquefied natural gas prices climb in tandem with those of oil.

items.title: Police foil petrol smuggling bid at Bukit Kayu Hitam, five arrested
items.summary: JITRA: Police have foiled an attempt to smuggle about 2,000 litres of petrol after intercepting five pickup trucks at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the operation, carried out by a team from the Kubang Pasu district police headquarters, took place within an hour from 7am yesterday. "Initial investigations found that the suspects used pickup trucks modified with additional steel tanks.

items.title: Steel Hawk secures three-year maintenance contract in Pengerang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Steel Hawk Bhd has secured a term contract for general maintenance of workshop machining at the Centralised Maintenance Workshop in the Pengerang Integrated Complex in Johor. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the three-year contract was awarded to its unit, Steel Hawk Engineering Sdn Bhd, by PRPC Utilities & Facilities Sdn Bhd. Steel Hawk will provide specified services as and when required throughout the contract period," it said.

items.title: Lynas expands rare earth output in Malaysia with early samarium oxide production
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Australia’s Lynas Rare Earths said today it had started producing samarium oxide at its Malaysian plant, bolstering its role as a key alternative to China for supplies of critical minerals. Samarium oxide, a heavy rare earth used in high-performance magnets in advanced industries such as electronics and aerospace, is being produced at Lynas’s facility in Gebeng, eastern Malaysia. She said the addition expands Lynas’s range of light and heavy rare earths used to manufacture high-performance permanent magnets.

items.title: Stocks slump on worsening war in Middle East, frail yen in focus
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Stocks slid and oil prices rose sharply on Thursday after a major escalation in the US and Israel's war with Iran rattled investors, while the Bank of Japan became the latest central bank to warn about the impact of energy costs on inflation. The hits to energy infrastructure sent shockwaves across markets as US crude futures extended gains above US$97 per barrel. Natural gas surged three per cent, while Brent futures rose to US$111.87 a barrel, up four per cent on the day.

items.title: Fugitive Tamim Dahri offers to surrender if four temples are demolished
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak, who is currently sought by police and believed to be in Saudi Arabia, has offered to surrender on the condition that authorities demolish four specific, allegedly illegal, Hindu temples in Malaysia. The post listed the four locations targeted for demolition as a temple on Jalan Pandanmas, another within the Bukit Mertajam Hospital compound, the site of the former Seafield Sri Maha Mariamman Temple, and a temple near the Jakel building in the city. “Why these four locations?

items.title: MCMC, telcos step up measures to ensure uninterrupted services during Aidilfitri
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), together with telecommunications service providers, is taking proactive measures to strengthen communication networks nationwide ahead of Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations. In a statement today, MCMC said the effort was aimed at ensuring the public continued to enjoy seamless and stable communication services, including voice calls, messaging, Internet browsing, and video connectivity, particularly when returning to their hometowns. It said that the measures focused on locations at high risk of network congestion, including major highways, rest and service areas, lay-bys, tourist destinations and densely populated areas.

items.title: DOSM: Malaysia’s inflation eases to 1.4pc, with eight categories recording softer gains
items.summary: These included insurance and financial services at 4.7 per cent (January 2026: 5.5 per cent), education 2.8 per cent (3.2 per cent), restaurant and accommodation services 2.5 per cent (3.0 per cent), and food and beverages 1.3 per cent (1.5 per cent). Other groups showed marginal increases, with health rising 1.2 per cent (1.4 per cent), housing, water, electricity, gas, and other fuels up 1.1 per cent (1.2 per cent), and information and communication advancing 0.5 per cent (0.7 per cent). Meanwhile, personal care, social protection, and miscellaneous goods and services increased to 6.9 per cent (6.6 per cent), while alcoholic beverages and tobacco rose to 2.6 per cent (2.5 per cent).

items.title: Govt maintains high flood‑readiness level ahead of Aidilfitri, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — Operational readiness for flood risk management during the Northeast Monsoon (MTL) remains a key government priority to safeguard public safety, especially with Hari Raya Aidilfitri approaching, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said PETRA has implemented round-the-clock flood forecasting and warning systems across 36 river basins, supported by 1,798 telemetry hydrological stations. “To date, 64 flood alerts and 39 warning notices have been issued,” he said.

items.title: Man charged over armed gang robbery at Bangsar clinic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — A lorry driver pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of committing gang robbery while armed with a parang at an ayurvedic clinic in Bangsar earlier this month. P. Sangara Lingam, 49, made the plea after the charge was read out to him before Judge Suhaila Haron, Free Malaysia Today reported. The charge was framed under Section 395 of the Penal Code for gang robbery, read together with Section 397 for armed robbery.

items.title: Ex-senior manager claims trial to two counts of receiving RM2.1m in stolen funds in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, March 19 — A former senior manager of a multinational company claimed trial to two counts of dishonestly receiving stolen funds amounting to RM2.1 million belonging to an Indonesian company three years ago. For the first charge, Vemal was accused of receiving RM735,294.11 into his Maybank account at a branch along Jalan Sultan Idris Shah here on July 26, 2023, knowing the funds belonged to PT Steelindo Wahana Perkasa, a subsidiary of Kuala Lumpur Kepong Berhad. For the second charge, he was accused of receiving RM1,447,521.87 into his bank account under similar circumstances at the same location on August 15, 2023.

items.title: Nine-month jail for man who filmed himself stepping on Quran
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 13 — The Magistrate’s Court today sentenced a man to nine months’ imprisonment for stepping on the Quran and uploading the act online last month. Magistrate Teoh Shu Yee handed down the sentence on Amir Abdul Malik, 41, after he pleaded guilty to the offence. Amir was charged with desecrating Islam by recording and uploading a video of himself stepping on the Quran with the intent to insult the religion at Kampung Durian Daun, Masjid Tanah, on February 28 this year.

items.title: IGP: Stay calm, avoid inflaming tensions over unauthorised houses of worship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail today urged Malaysians to remain calm over the issue of unauthorised houses of worship, warning that rising rhetoric risks inflaming racial tensions if left unchecked. He said what is fundamentally an administrative matter involving land ownership, local authority approvals and compliance with planning laws has increasingly spilled into the public sphere, where it is framed along religious and racial lines. Efforts like this help ensure racial harmony and national stability,” he said.

items.title: IGP: Activist Tamim may have fled to Saudi Arabia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The police claimed that activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak may have fled to Saudi Arabia and are actively tracking his whereabouts, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said checks indicated Tamim had travelled to Thailand and is now believed to be in Saudi Arabia. Earlier this week, Mohd Khalid confirmed that four individuals — including Zamri Vinoth and activist Arun Dorasamy — would be charged in connection with alleged religious provocation.

items.title: IGP: Police to record statements from 14 more individuals in alleged plot to topple govt probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Police may soon call in up to 14 more individuals to assist in investigations into an alleged attempt to topple the government and acts of sabotage against national stability, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said police expect to record statements from all identified individuals as part of ongoing investigations. “Investigations are still ongoing and the police are examining the matter while carrying out several follow-up actions.

items.title: New Sungai Krian Bridge opens, ending long wait for Pusa and Kabong residents, says Uggah
items.summary: KUCHING, March 19 — The long wait for residents of Pusa and Kabong to cross Sungai Krian has ended with the official opening of the Sungai Krian Bridge yesterday. Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas (5th right) receives a memento from Cassidy after the Sungai Krian Bridge soft launch ceremony March 19, 2026. — Picture via Facebook/JKR Sarawak Meanwhile, the Public Works Department (JKR) Sarawak said the Sungai Krian Bridge project, part of the Sarawak Coastal Road under Package D4, was executed with a contract value of RM112.5 million.

items.title: Culvert installation underway to tackle flooding at Jalan Hua Lai, Jalan Hock Ming in Bawang Assan, says SUPP
items.summary: SIBU, March 19 — Sarawak United Peoples’ Party (SUPP) Bawang Assan branch has initiated an infrastructure improvement project along Jalan Hua Lai and Jalan Hock Ming, involving the installation of culverts. The branch’s chairman Cr Kevin Lau said the project aims to enhance the local drainage system and address long-standing flooding issues faced by residents in the area. The affected roads have long suffered from poor drainage, resulting in frequent flooding during heavy rainfall.

items.title: Businessman Victor Chin accuses police of ‘unguided bombardment’ in AMLA probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Businessman Victor Chin Boon Long today renewed his criticism of the Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) Anti-Money Laundering (AMLA) unit, accusing it of “persecuting innocent people” and heavy-handedness in its investigation against him. “Anyone who knows me becomes collateral damage,” Chin claimed. “We are living in an environment where anyone who knows Victor Chin or calls Victor Chin their boss, gets AMLA-ed.

items.title: ETS tickets 86pc already sold as KTMB boosts trips for Raya travel
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 19 — A total of 217,000 Electric Train Service (ETS) tickets for the Hari Raya Aidilfitri travel period have been sold as of 9 am today, out of 257,000 tickets offered. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) Chief Operating Officer Afzar Zakariya said the figure represents about 86 per cent of total ticket sales, with around 35,000 tickets still available, mainly for trips into Kuala Lumpur. “It can be said that tickets for balik kampung journeys are almost gone, while tickets for trips into Kuala Lumpur are still available,” he said after inspecting operations at JB Sentral today.

items.title: Natural Resources Ministry pledges support for family of border survey officer swept away in Sarawak river, says Arthur Kurup
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) has assured that the welfare of the family of a Department of Survey and Mapping Malaysia (JUPEM) officer who went missing in Sungai Pandaruan, Limbang, will remain a priority throughout the ongoing search and rescue operation. Minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said he has instructed the department to extend all necessary assistance, including moral support and welfare coordination, to the victim’s family. Arthur said he had been informed of the tragic incident involving a JUPEM officer who was reportedly swept away by strong currents while carrying out boundary surveying work along the Malaysia–Brunei border at Sungai Pandaruan.

items.title: Fire razes 10 homes in Lutong, Sarawak; 42 homeless as firefighters battle dense layout (VIDEO)
items.summary: MIRI, March 19 — A devastating fire at the Kampung Pasir squatter colony in Lutong here early this morning has left 42 villagers homeless. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) said it received an emergency call at 4.06am and dispatched firefighters from the Lutong fire station, assisted by teams from Lopeng and Miri Central. “The fire eventually razed 10 houses to the ground, nine of which were occupied, displacing families comprising 42 individuals.

items.title: Perlis’ Hari Raya Aidilfitri @ Istana Arau celebration set for first day of Syawal
items.summary: KANGAR, March 19 — The Hari Raya Aidilfitri @ Istana Arau celebration at Istana Arau will be held at Dataran Majlis, Istana Arau, from 9.30am to noon on the first day of Syawal after the Aidilfitri prayer. It said Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, will also perform the Aidilfitri prayer, scheduled to begin at 8 am, at Dataran Istiadat Istana Arau. Attendees are advised to bring their own prayer mats, arrive in a state of ablution or perform ablution beforehand at the Arau State Mosque, and bring shoe bags for convenience.

items.title: Penang childcare worker charged with murder of four‑month‑old boy at HSJ centre
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 19 — A childcare centre worker was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a four-month-old baby boy. Nur Farhana Fazmar Abdullah, 22, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Nurul Rasyidah Mohd Akit, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Siti Nur Amalina Harun, while lawyer Muhaimin Hashim represented the accused.

items.title: Happiness rising: Malaysia outpaces Singapore and global average in latest Ipsos study
items.summary: Malaysia's improved score places it comfortably above the global average of 74 per cent, reflecting a broadly positive national mood despite international uncertainties. Most Southeast Asian nations recorded similar gains, with Indonesia topping the global charts at 85 per cent, the highest happiness score of any country measured. High-income Malaysians reported the strongest happiness outcomes, with 87 per cent describing themselves as happy — ten points above the global high-income average of 79 per cent.

items.title: Melaka crime index up 2.9pc to 318 cases, violent crimes rise 6.3pc
items.summary: MELAKA, March 19 — Crime index in Melaka rose 2.9 per cent to 318 cases from January 1 to March 16 compared to 309 cases in the same period last year. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said of the total, violent crimes including rape and causing injury increased by 6.3 per cent to 67 cases in the same period this year compared to 63 cases last year. “Likewise, property crimes have also increased by two per cent to 251 cases in the same period this year compared to 246 cases last year, which involves an increase in theft and motorcycle theft cases,” he told reporters here this morning.
